name: Altos de Bikotz Gane and Sarasola — circular from Basauri
name_original: Altos de Bikotz Gane y Sarasola — circular desde Basauri
region_intro: Road cycling around Basauri, located within Spain's Basque Country, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The area is characterized by its position at the confluence of the Nervión and Ibaizabal rivers, providing flatter sections, while the land quickly rises to features like Malmasín mountain at 361 meters. This varied topography, combining riverine plains and ascending routes, integrates Basauri into a broader cycling network. The region provides a range of road cycling routes suitable for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Basauri The most popular road cycling route is Altos de Bikotz Gane and Sarasola — circular from Basauri, a 35.6 miles (57.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and scenic ascents. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Dima Village and Scenic Views – Alto de Dima loop from Bidebieta-Basauri, a difficult 62.3 miles (100.3 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and expansive views. Local road cyclists also love the Alto de Sarasola – Arteako Benta loop from Basauri, a 28.1 miles (45.1 km) trail leading through rolling hills, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around Basauri is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and urban connections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more demanding routes. The routes in Basauri are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 1,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Basauri's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Basauri?
A: Basauri offers a good selection of road cycling routes, with nearly 20 distinct tours available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Basauri?
A: The terrain around Basauri is quite varied. You'll find flatter sections along the Nervión and Ibaizabal river valleys, ideal for easier rides. However, the landscape quickly rises, featuring rolling hills and ascents towards peaks like Malmasín mountain (361 meters), providing plenty of opportunities for challenging climbs and scenic views.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in Basauri?
A: Yes, the Basauri area, nestled within the broader Bizkaia region, is known for its challenging climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38286534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dima Village and Scenic Views – Alto de Dima loop from Bidebieta-Basauri&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 100 km path, feature significant elevation gains, offering a true test for experienced riders. The region's diverse topography ensures plenty of demanding ascents.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Basauri?
A: Absolutely. Many road cycling routes around Basauri are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032231750/altos-de-bikotz-gane-and-sarasola-circular-from-basauri" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altos de Bikotz Gane and Sarasola — circular from Basauri&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain and scenic ascents in a convenient circular format.

Q: What are some scenic landmarks or viewpoints I can see while road cycling near Basauri?
A: While cycling around Basauri, you can enjoy scenic views of the river valleys and surrounding hills. For specific landmarks, consider routes that pass near Bilbao, where you can find attractions like the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/335425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guggenheim Museum&lt;/a&gt;, the elegant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2911339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zubizuri Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4628937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;. The region also offers views of various mountain peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5322343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Pagasarri&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Basauri?
A: While many routes offer moderate to difficult challenges, Basauri's location at the confluence of the Nervión and Ibaizabal rivers provides flatter sections suitable for easier rides. Look for routes that stick to the river valleys or the new bike path connecting Basauri and Bilbao for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Basauri?
A: The road cycling routes in Basauri are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 25 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, which combines river valleys with rolling hills, and the excellent network that connects Basauri to the broader cycling-rich Basque Country.

Q: Is it possible to combine road cycling with exploring nearby towns or villages?
A: Yes, Basauri is well-integrated into the regional cycling network, making it easy to connect to other towns and villages. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dima Village and Scenic Views – Alto de Dima loop from Ariz&lt;/a&gt; allow you to cycle through picturesque areas and visit charming locations like Dima Village, experiencing the local culture and landscapes.

Q: Are there options for shorter road cycling routes, perhaps under 3 hours?
A: Yes, you can find several shorter road cycling routes around Basauri. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032231750/altos-de-bikotz-gane-and-sarasola-circular-from-basauri" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altos de Bikotz Gane and Sarasola — circular from Basauri&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 57 km route that typically takes under 3 hours to complete. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31342693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Sarasola – Arteako Benta loop from Basauri&lt;/a&gt; is even shorter at 45 km, often completed in about 2 hours.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Basauri?
A: The Basque Country generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be warm, but coastal breezes can make riding enjoyable. It's always a good idea to check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: How can I find more information about cycling in the wider Bizkaia region?
A: For more comprehensive information about cyclotourism in the broader Bizkaia region, where Basauri is located, you can visit the official tourism website. It provides details on various routes and cycling opportunities across the province: &lt;a href="https://www.visitbiscay.eus/en/-/cyclotourisme-en-bizkaia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitbiscay.eus&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that connect Basauri to Bilbao?
A: Yes, a new bike path has been developed connecting Basauri and Bilbao, partially following an old tram route. This path enhances accessibility and provides a safe way for cyclists to travel between the two urban areas, allowing easy access to the larger network of routes around Bilbao.
tips: The Aranguren tower house is located in the municipality of Orozco (Barrio de Aranguren), province of Vizcaya, Basque Country. The tower stands on the banks of the Arnauri River, next to the road that connects the capital of the municipality with a series of neighborhoods: Ibarra, Zaloa, Urgoiti, all in the foothills of Gorbea.
